news.headline: WGS Stock Slumps After Hours As Firm Predicts Lower Test Volume For Year
news.summary: GeneDx shares fell after hours after the company signaled softer full-year exome and genome test volume than previously expected. The warning deepened demand concerns first flagged with the Q1 miss and is the freshest negative operational catalyst heading into the Q2 print.

news.headline: GeneDx Holdings (WGS) Hit with Securities Class Action Following 49% Stock Collapse
news.summary: Multiple law firms filed and publicized securities class actions alleging GeneDx misled investors about the value and synergies of its Fabric Genomics acquisition before a roughly $31 million impairment (~94% of the purchase price). Shareholders who bought between April 16, 2025 and May 4, 2026 have until August 3, 2026 to seek lead-plaintiff status, keeping legal risk an active overhang.

news.headline: GeneDx price target raised to $63 from $50 at Piper Sandler on coverage policy
news.summary: Piper Sandler lifted its price target to $63 from $50 after Carelon codified outpatient genome coverage, calling it a structural ASP de-risking event. The firm estimates the policy could add roughly $100 of incremental average selling price by the second half of 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

news.headline: GeneDx Names Mark Gardner President
news.summary: GeneDx appointed Mark Gardner as President, effective June 15, 2026, reporting to CEO Katherine Stueland to lead commercial and operations at scale. The hire came amid the guidance reset and litigation, positioning new management to rebuild execution credibility.

news.headline: Carelon adds outpatient genome sequencing coverage, lifting insured coverage to ~80%
news.summary: Carelon, the largest U.S. lab benefit manager, added genome sequencing under the same clinical criteria as exome for undiagnosed rare disease, raising insured genome coverage to nearly 80%. It is a structural volume and reimbursement tailwind and arguably the most important positive catalyst of the period, underpinning the view that the sell-off went too far.

news.headline: Hedge Funder Eli Casdin Is Loading Up on GeneDx Shares
news.summary: Casdin Capital bought roughly 690,000 GeneDx shares for about $32.6 million over recent weeks, becoming the largest institutional holder with 3.7 million shares. The board-linked specialist's open-market accumulation into the crash is read as a contrarian vote of confidence against the litigation-driven sell-off.

catalysts.label: Warrant expiration (WGSWW)
catalysts.detail: Expiration of GeneDx warrants on July 22, 2026, a technical factor for capital structure and potential dilution.

catalysts.label: Class-action lead-plaintiff deadline
catalysts.detail: Deadline for shareholders who bought between April 16, 2025 and May 4, 2026 to move to lead the consolidated securities fraud litigation tied to the Fabric Genomics impairment and guidance cut.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (est.)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; the market is watching test volume, revised full-year guidance ($475-490M), and Fabric Genomics cleanup after the July preannouncement of softer volume.

catalysts.label: New President execution
catalysts.detail: Mark Gardner joined as President; the market will watch whether new leadership stabilizes volume trends and rebuilds guidance credibility.

catalysts.label: Reimbursement coverage expansion / ASP uplift
catalysts.detail: Further payer and Medicaid coverage decisions following Carelon could support genome/exome volume; Piper Sandler sees ~$100 incremental ASP by H2 2027 as the exome-to-genome mix shifts.

pulse: GeneDx remains headline-driven and high-risk after a Q1 miss, a full-year guidance cut to $475-490M, and a ~49% crash from its early-June record, driven by a ~94% write-off on the Fabric Genomics acquisition. That sparked a wave of securities class actions with an August 3 lead-plaintiff deadline, and a July 10 warning of lower full-year test volume added fresh downside pressure. Offsetting the negatives, Carelon lifted insured genome coverage to ~80%, Piper Sandler raised its target to $63 citing structural ASP de-risking, specialist Eli Casdin has been aggressively accumulating, and new President Mark Gardner is tasked with restoring execution ahead of the early-August Q2 print.
